description_long: This object is a wooden easel, typically used for holding canvases or boards for painting, drawing, or display. It features an A-frame structure with two main front legs and a rear support, all made from light-colored wood with a visible grain pattern. The top section includes a horizontal crossbar, and there are metal fasteners or bolts visible at the joints, suggesting some adjustability or foldability. The lower section has a horizontal support bar that helps stabilize the frame. The overall design is simple, functional, and sturdy, suitable for outdoor or indoor urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or art fairs. The surface appears matte and slightly textured, with a natural wood finish.
description: A light wood A-frame easel with visible grain, metal fasteners, and horizontal support bars, designed for holding canvases or boards. The structure is sturdy and functional, with a matte, natural finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the easel, both main legs, and the horizontal support bars.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the A-frame profile, the depth of the structure, and the metal fastener at the joint.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the back supports and horizontal bars.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the A-frame profile and the metal fastener.
